description: Open 2D/2H/2S with exactly 6 cards and 5-11 HCP; responder uses support-driven raises and Feature 2NT
purpose: Preempt opponents with a disciplined six-card weak two while letting responder use support-based raises or a Feature 2NT ask to judge game
scopeNote: Opener's weak-2 in D / H / S (5-11 HCP, 6-card suit) plus Larry-standard responder actions are in scope: Feature `2NT`, forcing new suits (authored at `hand.hcp >= 14` with opener rebids), support-driven raises, the narrow Hand-5 evaluation game raise with exactly 3-card major support, and responder continuations after Feature replies. The Feature ask is authored at `bridge.totalPointsForRaise >= 14`; 3-card supported invitational hands stay with 2NT below the direct-game threshold, while `bridge.totalPointsForRaise >= 16` places game by evaluation. Ogust asks and vulnerability-sensitive opening-range tuning are deliberately not supported - a separate variant would be required. Suit-quality is encoded as 'two of top three or three of top five'.

snapshot.text:
# Responding to Weak 2-bids

By: Larry Cohen

If you've been with me all year, you are now reading the 12th article in this series. Last article we started with weak two bids. This month we respond—probably my most favorite (and famous) topic in bridge.

## Responding to a weak 2-bid

1. Possession of 13 points is no big deal; partner has fewer than 13. If you don't have at least 3-card support, and you have 13 HCP or fewer, you should pass partner's weak two-bid.

2. Support with support. You don't need points to raise partner. Surely, most of my readers have heard of the LAW of Total Tricks. With 3 trumps, raise partner's weak two to the 3-level (if you don't have game interest). With 4 trumps, raise to the 4-level. To read all the why's and why-not's, click here.

3. With an invitational hand, responder should bid 2NT to ask opener if he is minimum or not. Opener rebids his suit with a minimum; with more he identifies his outside Conventional 2NT response to a weak two-bid that asks opener to show a high card in a side suit if he is not with a minimum preempt feature (usually an A or K). This is called "Feature." There are a small minority of players who use a different conventional response to the 2NT bid, typically Ogust.

4. New suits by responder–forcing is Standard. Make sure you agree with your partner.

## Practice Problems - Responding to 2♥

**Hand 1:**
♠ A 6 5 4
♥ 2
♦ K J 10 4
♣ A J 5 4

**Answer:** Pass. You are high enough.

**Hand 2:**
♠ 8 7 6
♥ K 5 4
♦ Q 9 7 6 5
♣ 4 3

**Answer:** 3♥. Follow the "LAW". With 9-trumps, raise to the 3-level.

**Hand 3:**
♠ 2
♥ K Q 4 3
♦ A Q J 5 3
♣ K 5 3

**Answer:** 4♥. One, you have 10 trumps, and two, you expect to make it.

**Hand 4:**
♠ 8 3
♥ 8
♦ A K Q J 9 7 6
♣ A K 4

**Answer:** 3♦. Make sure partner and you have agreed that new suits are forcing.

**Hand 5:**
♠ A 6 5
♥ K 7 2
♦ A K 9 7 6
♣ K 3

**Answer:** 4♥. Where you think you belong (don't use the "LAW" for games/slams).

## After Partner Responds 2NT to our Weak 2-Bid

- Opener's first responsibility is to clarify whether or not her hand is minimum. With a minimum hand, she rebids 3 of her suit. Note: Minimum or maximum depends on position, vulnerability and suit quality, but in general, 6-8 is minimum and 9-11 is maximum.

- With more than a minimum, she shows an outside feature, normally an ace or king. She is also welcome to find another bid if pressed. With a maximum and no Conventional 2NT response to a weak two-bid that asks opener to show a high card in a side suit if he is not with a minimum preempt feature (thus a very good trump suit), bid 3NT.

## Practice Problems - Opening 2♦ (nobody vul.) and Partner Bids 2NT

**Hand 6:**
♠ K 5 4
♥ 10 7 6
♦ Q J 10 8 7 5
♣ 2

**Answer:** 3♦. Holding a minimum, opener should not show a feature.

**Hand 7:**
♠ 10 4 3
♥ 2
♦ K Q J 9 7 6
♣ K 5 4

**Answer:** 3♣. With a maximum, show the feature.

**Hand 8:**
♠ A 4
♥ 8 6 2
♦ K Q 10 8 3 2
♣ 8 7

**Answer:** 3♠. Another maximum; show the feature.

**Hand 9:**
♠ 9 5
♥ 8 6 5 2
♦ A K Q 10 9 7
♣ 2

**Answer:** 3NT. A maximum without a feature—3NT shows something like this.

**Hand 10:**
♠ Q 10 4
♥ 8 6 5 2
♦ K J 10 9 8 4
♣ --

**Answer:** 3♦. This is a minimum (sort of).

teaching.tradeoff: Using 2D/2H/2S as weak openings means you can't use them for strong two-bids.
teaching.principle: Weak twos preempt first, then let responder decide whether fit-based pressure or a Feature ask is the right way to continue.

commonMistakes.text: Do not open a Weak Two with anything other than a real six-card suit.
commonMistakes.reason: This module teaches Larry's common agreement that the opening suit is exactly six cards, so stretching on five or upgrading to seven distorts the picture partner expects.

commonMistakes.text: Do not treat a direct raise as an invitation.
commonMistakes.reason: In this treatment direct raises are support-driven pressure bids; use 2NT when you need opener to clarify whether the weak two is minimum, feature-rich, or solid.

commonMistakes.text: Do not play Ogust step replies in this module.
commonMistakes.reason: This fixture is pure Feature: rebid the weak-two suit with a minimum, show a side ace or king with extras, or rebid 3NT with a solid trump suit.

commonMistakes.text: Do not forget that a new suit response is forcing for one round.
commonMistakes.reason: Larry treats a new suit and 2NT alike as game-interest actions, so responder must be prepared to bid again after starting with a new suit.

Open 2H: exactly 6 hearts, weak-two values, and reasonable suit quality
Feature 2NT over 2H: ask opener whether the weak two is minimum or holds a useful side feature
New suit forcing over 2H: natural game-interest action with a real side suit
3H raise: support-driven further preempt when you are not using the Feature ask
4H raise: jump with 4+ trumps either to apply pressure or to place the game directly
4H evaluation raise: with exactly three hearts and enough total points, bid game by evaluation rather than by the LAW
Pass over 2H: no fit-based raise and no Feature-game interest
Rebid 3H: minimum weak two, whatever the side honors look like
Show a clubs feature over 2H-2NT: non-minimum hand with an outside ace or king
Show a diamonds feature over 2H-2NT: non-minimum hand with an outside ace or king
Show a spades feature over 2H-2NT: non-minimum hand with an outside ace or king
Rebid 3NT over 2H-2NT: non-minimum hand with a very good hearts suit and no side feature to show
Open 2S: exactly 6 spades, weak-two values, and reasonable suit quality
Feature 2NT over 2S: ask opener whether the weak two is minimum or holds a useful side feature
New suit forcing over 2S: natural game-interest action with a real side suit
3S raise: support-driven further preempt when you are not using the Feature ask
4S raise: jump with 4+ trumps either to apply pressure or to place the game directly
4S evaluation raise: with exactly three spades and enough total points, bid game by evaluation rather than by the LAW

Rebid 3S: minimum weak two, whatever the side honors look like

Rebid 3NT over 2S-2NT: non-minimum hand with a very good spades suit and no side feature to show

teachingLabel.summary: Return to opener's suit when the feature helps but the hand still belongs below game

clauses.description: No long clubs suit to play for game

teachingLabel.name: Pass

clauses.description: Enough high-card strength to convert the feature into game

teachingLabel.name: Bid 4H
teachingLabel.summary: Bid game in the suit opener just featured when that is clearly the best strain

definitions.description: Hearts suit quality passes authority: 2 of top 3 honors (A/K/Q) OR 3 of top 5 honors (A/K/Q/J/T)
Spades suit quality passes authority: 2 of top 3 honors (A/K/Q) OR 3 of top 5 honors (A/K/Q/J/T)
Diamonds suit quality passes authority: 2 of top 3 honors (A/K/Q) OR 3 of top 5 honors (A/K/Q/J/T)
Opener is minimum for the Feature structure (5-7 NV, 6-7 vul)
HCP in weak two opening range (5-11 NV, 6-11 vul)
Hearts suit is solid enough for the 3NT feature reply (AKQ in trump)
Spades suit is solid enough for the 3NT feature reply (AKQ in trump)
Diamonds suit is solid enough for the 3NT feature reply (AKQ in trump)
Has an outside clubs feature led by an ace or king
Has an outside diamonds feature led by an ace or king
Has an outside hearts feature led by an ace or king
Has an outside spades feature led by an ace or king
Has 5+ cards in a suit other than hearts
Has 5+ cards in a suit other than spades
Has 5+ cards in a suit other than diamonds

summaryCard.trigger: You open a weak two, partner responds
summaryCard.partnership: This module teaches Larry Cohen's standard Feature treatment: direct raises are support-driven and two notrump is the game-interest ask.

whenToUse.gloss: Open two diamonds with a disciplined six-card suit, weak-two values, and the intent to preempt first and describe later.
Open two hearts with the same weak-two hand type when hearts are the disciplined six-card suit.
Open two spades when the spade suit is exactly six cards and the hand belongs in the weak-two range.

workedAuctions.label: Feature ask locates the best game

calls.rationale: Opener shows a standard weak two in hearts.
Responder has invitational or stronger values and asks for a Feature reply.
The 3C reply shows a non-minimum hand with a club feature.
Knowing where opener's help lies, responder can place the contract in the right game strain.

workedAuctions.label: Counter-example: direct raises are not invitations

calls.rationale: North opens a weak two in spades.
This is just a support-driven further preempt, not a way to invite game.
Once responder has furthered the preempt directly, the partnership is done describing the hand.

items.ourAction: Keep the weak-two meaning and compete naturally according to your partnership's agreements.
items.note: Larry's standard notes that fancy redouble gadgets are outside the core treatment.

items.ourAction: Compete naturally; once space is gone, the clean Feature-ask structure is no longer the focus.
items.note: Simple raises still show support, while cue bids and other competitive tools become partnership matters.

items.opponentAction: No interference after partner's weak two opening
items.ourAction: Use two notrump as the Feature ask and treat a new suit as a one-round force.
items.note: This fixture does not include Ogust step replies.

items.recommendation: With no game interest, pass or make the support-driven further raise with four trumps.
items.note: Direct raises are obstructive, not invitational.

items.recommendation: With invitational or stronger values but no clear contract, bid two notrump as the Feature ask.
items.note: Responder is asking whether opener is minimum, has a side ace or king, or has a solid suit for three notrump.

items.recommendation: With a clear destination, place the contract directly instead of asking first.
items.note: The feature structure helps when strain or level is still unclear.

relatedLinks.discriminator: Strong two clubs uses the same level for the opposite hand type: forcing power instead of a preempt.
Choose a natural opening when the hand is too balanced, too strong, or too short in the suit for a weak two.

teaching.purpose: Open light hands with a disciplined six-card suit at the 2-level, then let responder use support-driven raises or a Feature 2NT ask to judge game.
teaching.whenToUse: Open 2D/2H/2S with exactly six cards in the suit and 5-11 HCP. As responder, make a direct support raise with trump support, or use 2NT as the Feature ask with invitational or stronger values when the final strain is still unclear.
whenNotToUse: 2C is reserved for strong openings — never open 2C as a weak two
With a hand too strong for a weak two (12+ HCP) — open at the 1-level instead
After opponent interference — standard competitive actions apply
teaching.tradeoff: 2-level openings lose their natural strong meaning in favor of disciplined preemptive action.
teaching.principle: Weak twos obstruct the opponents first; the Feature ask lets responder decide whether the hand belongs in partscore, game, or a different strain.
teaching.roles: Opener describes the quality of the weak two; responder captains once 2NT asks for a Feature reply.
